channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Fully Funded EM-SLAM PhD Studentship 2026 in the UK: Research Sustainable Living, Climate Anxiety, and Mental Wellbeing
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The East Midlands Sustainable Living and Mental Wellbeing (EM-SLAM) Doctoral Focal Award offers a fully funded PhD opportunity for students interested in exploring the intersection between mental health, sustainable living, and climate-related social challenges. This prestigious doctoral program is supported by the Arts and Humanities Research Council (AHRC) and brings together several leading UK universities […]

The post Fully Funded EM-SLAM PhD Studentship 2026 in the UK: Research Sustainable Living, Climate Anxiety, and Mental Wellbeing appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The East Midlands Sustainable Living and Mental Wellbeing (EM-SLAM) Doctoral Focal Award offers a fully funded PhD opportunity for students interested in exploring the intersection between mental health, sustainable living, and climate-related social challenges. This prestigious doctoral program is supported by the Arts and Humanities Research Council (AHRC) and brings together several leading UK universities and regional partners to train the next generation of researchers, policymakers, and creative practitioners.

The program will begin in 2026 and is open to both UK and international applicants. It provides four years of financial support, advanced training, and opportunities to collaborate with organizations across the health, sustainability, arts, and community sectors.

Applicants must submit their applications before March 31, 2026.

About the EM-SLAM Programme

The East Midlands Sustainable Living and Mental Wellbeing (EM-SLAM) initiative is a collaborative research program designed to address the growing psychological and social challenges associated with climate change and ecological uncertainty.

The program is jointly delivered by a partnership of universities across the East Midlands region of the United Kingdom, including:

  • Loughborough University

  • De Montfort University

  • University of Leicester

  • Nottingham Trent University

These institutions work together with a range of non-academic partners, including organizations involved in public health, environmental sustainability, community development, and the creative arts.

The primary aim of EM-SLAM is to explore how societies can support individuals and communities in living sustainably while maintaining mental wellbeing and social resilience in the face of environmental crises.

Through interdisciplinary research and storytelling approaches, the program seeks to generate knowledge that can inform policy, inspire communities, and support healthier relationships between people and the environment.

Research Focus: Sustainable Living and Mental Wellbeing

One of the defining characteristics of the EM-SLAM doctoral program is its focus on the intersection between mental health and environmental sustainability.

Climate change and ecological disruption are increasingly affecting the psychological wellbeing of individuals and communities. Concepts such as climate anxiety, ecological grief, and environmental distress are becoming more prominent in academic research and public discourse.

The EM-SLAM programme aims to investigate these issues through innovative research that highlights human experiences, narratives, and community perspectives.

Doctoral researchers will explore how people can:

  • Adapt to ecological uncertainty while maintaining mental resilience

  • Build sustainable lifestyles that support both environmental health and human wellbeing

  • Develop meaningful connections with nature and community

  • Address climate anxiety and ecological grief through storytelling and social engagement

Researchers are encouraged to develop projects that incorporate qualitative and interpretative research methods, with storytelling serving as a central framework for understanding lived experiences.

Interdisciplinary Research Environment

The EM-SLAM programme provides a highly interdisciplinary research environment that bridges multiple academic fields.

Students will receive supervision and mentorship from experts in areas such as:

  • Social sciences

  • Arts and humanities

  • Environmental studies

  • Public health

  • Cultural and creative industries

This interdisciplinary approach ensures that doctoral researchers develop a broad perspective on sustainability and mental health challenges while learning how to collaborate across different academic and professional disciplines.

Students will also work closely with external partners, allowing them to connect their research to real-world policy and community initiatives.

Why Apply for the EM-SLAM Doctoral Studentship

The EM-SLAM PhD studentship offers a wide range of academic and professional benefits designed to support early-career researchers.

Advanced Research Training

Students will gain advanced research skills through a comprehensive training programme that includes methodological workshops, interdisciplinary seminars, and professional development activities.

This training is designed to prepare participants for careers in academia, policymaking, creative industries, and public sector organizations.

Collaborative Research Opportunities

Participants will work with researchers and professionals from different sectors to co-design projects that address real-world social and environmental challenges.

This collaborative approach allows doctoral researchers to develop research that is both academically rigorous and socially impactful.

Professional Placements and Partnerships

A key component of the EM-SLAM program is the opportunity to undertake short placements with partner organizations.

These placements may involve collaboration with:

  • Public health organizations

  • Environmental sustainability initiatives

  • Cultural and creative institutions

  • Community development programs

Such experiences allow researchers to apply their academic work in practical settings and gain valuable professional experience.

Inclusive Research Community

Students will join a vibrant and supportive cohort of researchers from diverse backgrounds.

The programme prioritizes equity, diversity, and inclusion, actively encouraging applications from individuals who may be underrepresented in academia.

Funding and Financial Support

The EM-SLAM Doctoral Focal Award provides four years of fully funded doctoral support.

The funding package includes:

  • Full coverage of UK tuition fees

  • A tax-free stipend to support living expenses

  • Funding for training and professional development

  • Financial support for conference participation

  • Opportunities for research collaboration and placements

The programme is funded through the Arts and Humanities Research Council (AHRC), one of the UK’s most prestigious research funding bodies.

While the studentship primarily covers UK fees, international students are also eligible, although only a limited number of awards are available for applicants from outside the UK.

According to UK Research and Innovation guidelines, up to 30 percent of the studentships may be awarded to international candidates.

Entry Requirements

Applicants must demonstrate strong academic preparation and a clear research interest in the themes of sustainable living and mental wellbeing.

Typical requirements include:

  • A background in arts and humanities or social sciences

  • A Master’s degree in a relevant discipline

  • A first or upper second class honours undergraduate degree from a UK university or equivalent qualification

Applicants with a lower second class honours degree may still be considered if they hold a Master’s degree with Merit level or equivalent.

English Language Requirements

Applicants whose first language is not English must demonstrate proficiency through an approved English language test.

The required score is:

  • IELTS overall score of 6.5

  • Minimum 6.0 in each component (reading, writing, listening, and speaking)

For more information click here 

Discover more opportunities here

The post Fully Funded EM-SLAM PhD Studentship 2026 in the UK: Research Sustainable Living, Climate Anxiety, and Mental Wellbeing appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: AstraZeneca Young Health Programme Scholarships 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity to Attend the AFS Youth Assembly in Geneva
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The AstraZeneca Young Health Programme (YHP) Scholarships 2026 offer an exceptional opportunity for young leaders around the world to participate in the AFS Youth Assembly, one of the most influential global youth forums dedicated to sustainable development and international cooperation. The event will take place August 12–14, 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland, bringing together emerging leaders, […]

The post AstraZeneca Young Health Programme Scholarships 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity to Attend the AFS Youth Assembly in Geneva appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The AstraZeneca Young Health Programme (YHP) Scholarships 2026 offer an exceptional opportunity for young leaders around the world to participate in the AFS Youth Assembly, one of the most influential global youth forums dedicated to sustainable development and international cooperation.

The event will take place August 12–14, 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland, bringing together emerging leaders, policymakers, researchers, and youth advocates to collaborate on solutions to some of the most pressing global challenges.

This initiative is organized by AFS Intercultural Programs in partnership with AstraZeneca, through its Young Health Programme, which focuses on empowering youth to create healthier, more sustainable communities worldwide.

The scholarship aims to support young people who are already working to advance health equity, climate and nature action, and resilient health systems.

The application deadline has not been officially specified, so interested candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible once the application portal opens.

About the AstraZeneca Young Health Programme (YHP)

The Young Health Programme (YHP) is AstraZeneca’s global community investment initiative focused on preventing non-communicable diseases (NCDs) and promoting health equity, particularly among young people.

Through partnerships with civil society organizations, youth leaders, and international institutions, the program aims to empower the next generation to become advocates for better health systems and sustainable development.

By supporting youth initiatives, the program addresses the growing global health challenges associated with chronic diseases, environmental risks, and healthcare inequality.

The AstraZeneca Young Health Programme Scholarships are part of this broader mission to equip youth with the knowledge, networks, and leadership tools necessary to drive positive change.

About the AFS Youth Assembly

The AFS Youth Assembly is a global forum that convenes young leaders from around the world to discuss innovative solutions to international challenges.

Hosted in Geneva, Switzerland, a city known for its strong diplomatic presence and international institutions, the assembly provides a unique environment for dialogue, collaboration, and knowledge exchange.

The 2026 edition will gather youth advocates working across sectors including health, climate, sustainable development, education, and social justice.

Participants will have the opportunity to:

  • Engage in high-level discussions with global experts and policymakers

  • Participate in workshops and collaborative sessions focused on leadership and innovation

  • Share their experiences and projects with an international audience

  • Build a global network of like-minded changemakers

The Youth Assembly is designed to amplify youth voices in international decision-making processes and encourage collaborative solutions to global challenges.

Focus Areas of the AstraZeneca Scholarship

Applicants for the AstraZeneca Young Health Programme Scholarship are expected to demonstrate commitment and leadership in at least one of the following key areas:

Advancing Health Equity

One of the core priorities of the program is addressing inequalities in healthcare access and outcomes.

Scholars working in this area focus on preventing non-communicable diseases (NCDs) among young people. These diseases include:

  • Lung diseases

  • Cardiovascular diseases

  • Cancer

  • Type 2 diabetes

  • Chronic kidney disease

Young advocates supporting individuals living with rare diseases are also encouraged to apply.

The goal is to promote early prevention strategies and ensure equitable access to healthcare services across communities.

Climate and Nature Action

Climate change is increasingly recognized as a major threat to global health.

Young leaders working in climate and environmental advocacy can apply if their work focuses on reducing the harmful effects of climate change on public health and well-being.

Applicants may be involved in initiatives such as:

  • Climate awareness campaigns

  • Environmental health advocacy

  • Sustainable community programs

  • Climate policy engagement

These efforts are essential to protecting vulnerable populations and ensuring long-term health outcomes.

Building Resilient Health Systems

Another critical priority is strengthening health systems to respond to both current and future health challenges.

Applicants working in this field may focus on:

  • Strengthening healthcare infrastructure

  • Promoting early disease detection and prevention

  • Improving community-based healthcare programs

  • Advocating for policy reforms that enhance healthcare accessibility

Resilient health systems are essential to ensuring societies can respond effectively to both emerging diseases and ongoing health crises.

What the Scholarship Includes

Recipients of the AstraZeneca Young Health Programme Scholarship will receive support to attend the AFS Youth Assembly 2026 in Geneva.

The scholarship is designed to ensure that young leaders from diverse backgrounds can participate in the event regardless of financial barriers.

Benefits typically include:

  • Access to the AFS Youth Assembly global conference

  • Participation in leadership workshops and panel discussions

  • Networking opportunities with international experts and policymakers

  • Collaboration with youth leaders from around the world

Scholars also gain international exposure and recognition for their work in health, climate, and sustainability.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Today’s world faces a complex intersection of challenges including rising chronic disease, climate change, and unequal healthcare access.

These issues disproportionately affect young people and marginalized communities.

By supporting youth leaders who are actively addressing these problems, the AstraZeneca Young Health Programme seeks to build a generation capable of driving systemic change.

Participation in the Youth Assembly enables young leaders to:

  • Share innovative ideas and projects

  • Influence global discussions on health and sustainability

  • Develop leadership and advocacy skills

  • Build long-term collaborations with global changemakers

For many participants, the Youth Assembly becomes a launching platform for international careers in policy, research, public health, and global development.

How to Apply

Applicants interested in the AstraZeneca Young Health Programme Scholarship should prepare a strong application highlighting their work, leadership experience, and commitment to improving global health and sustainability.

Candidates should clearly demonstrate:

  • Their involvement in projects related to health equity, climate action, or resilient health systems

  • Their vision for creating positive change in their communities

  • Their motivation to participate in the Youth Assembly

Applications must be submitted through the official AFS Youth Assembly scholarship portal.

Prospective applicants are encouraged to regularly check the official website for application updates and submission guidelines.

For more information click here 

Apply here

Discover more opportunities here

The post AstraZeneca Young Health Programme Scholarships 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity to Attend the AFS Youth Assembly in Geneva appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: PhD Scholarship in Political Science at the University of Copenhagen: Researching Cancel Culture and Moral Judgement in Democratic Politics
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The University of Copenhagen (UCPH) is inviting applications for a prestigious PhD scholarship at the Department of Political Science focusing on the research project “Moralizing Politics: The Practice of Judging Others (MoPo)”. This unique doctoral opportunity allows researchers to explore one of the most debated phenomena in modern politics—cancel culture and the ethics of judging […]

The post PhD Scholarship in Political Science at the University of Copenhagen: Researching Cancel Culture and Moral Judgement in Democratic Politics appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of Copenhagen (UCPH) is inviting applications for a prestigious PhD scholarship at the Department of Political Science focusing on the research project “Moralizing Politics: The Practice of Judging Others (MoPo)”. This unique doctoral opportunity allows researchers to explore one of the most debated phenomena in modern politics—cancel culture and the ethics of judging others in democratic societies.

The selected candidate will enroll in the Faculty of Social Sciences under the Copenhagen Graduate School of Social Sciences, one of Europe’s leading academic environments for political science research. The position is expected to begin 1 August 2026 or as soon as possible thereafter, offering a fully funded doctoral pathway for scholars interested in normative political theory and democratic practice.

Application Deadline: April 13, 2026

About the MoPo Research Project

The PhD scholarship is part of the European Research Council–funded project “Moralizing Politics: The Practice of Judging Others (MoPo)”, led by Professor Christian Rostbøll at the University of Copenhagen.

The project examines a growing phenomenon in contemporary politics: the increasing tendency of citizens to judge others morally by labeling them as good or bad individuals based on political beliefs, behaviors, or social conduct. This process—often referred to as moralization in politics—can serve both constructive and destructive roles within democratic systems.

On one hand, moral judgment can strengthen democratic accountability by encouraging citizens to uphold ethical standards in political life. On the other hand, excessive moralization can fuel polarization, hostility, and social fragmentation, potentially undermining democratic dialogue.

To investigate this complex issue, the MoPo project combines philosophical analysis with empirical case studies across four domains:

  • Cancel culture

  • The dignity of work

  • Fake news

  • Climate shaming

Each case explores how moral judgments function within political practices and whether they ultimately benefit or harm democratic governance.

Focus of the PhD Project: Cancel Culture and Democratic Legitimacy

The PhD candidate selected for this scholarship will conduct research on the case study titled “Cancel Culture: Human Equality and Respect.”

Cancel culture has become a prominent political and social phenomenon, particularly in digital spaces and public discourse. It refers to the practice of calling out, blaming, shaming, or socially sanctioning individuals who are perceived to have violated fundamental moral principles about respect and equality.

Within the framework of the MoPo project, the doctoral researcher will examine the philosophical and political dimensions of cancel culture, including:

  • The reactive attitudes involved in moral condemnation, inspired by philosopher P.F. Strawson

  • The moral principles invoked when individuals or groups are “cancelled”

  • The relationship between cancel culture and deliberative democracy

  • Whether cancel culture can be considered a legitimate form of democratic political practice

The project will employ the method of practice-sensitive reflective equilibrium, integrating qualitative case studies of cancelling practices with moral philosophy and democratic theory. The doctoral candidate will also have the freedom to develop their own research design within this broader theoretical and methodological framework.

PhD Training and Academic Environment

The PhD program at the Faculty of Social Sciences provides rigorous research training and offers opportunities for students to develop strong academic and professional skills.

During the program, the doctoral researcher will:

  • Write a PhD dissertation on cancel culture and moral judgment in politics

  • Collaborate with other team members within the MoPo project

  • Contribute to joint academic publications

  • Participate in international workshops, conferences, and seminars

  • Take part in teaching activities and academic dissemination

The program can be pursued through two study structures:

5+3 PhD Program (Three Years)
Designed for candidates who already hold a relevant master’s degree. PhD students receive a salary based on agreements between the Danish Ministry of Finance and the Danish Confederation of Professional Associations.

4+4 PhD Program (Four Years)
Designed for candidates with a bachelor’s degree who continue directly into an integrated MSc and PhD program. Students receive state educational grants until the master’s degree is completed before transitioning into the salaried PhD phase.

Both tracks involve academic responsibilities such as teaching or research-related tasks.

Research Support and International Opportunities

The University of Copenhagen provides extensive support to doctoral researchers participating in the MoPo project. Funding is available for:

  • Conference participation

  • Summer school attendance

  • Field research

  • International research visits

These opportunities allow PhD students to build a strong international academic network and develop expertise in political theory and democratic studies.

Required Qualifications

Applicants should demonstrate strong academic preparation and research potential in political theory. Key qualifications include:

  • A master’s degree in political science, political theory, or practical/political philosophy

  • Demonstrated research ability in normative political theory

  • Strong academic writing skills

  • Interest in democratic practices and the intersection between political theory and political science

Additional desirable qualifications include:

  • Knowledge of qualitative research methods such as text analysis, interviews, or ethnographic research

  • Ability to work independently while collaborating with a research team

Applicants educated outside Denmark must hold qualifications equivalent to a Danish bachelor’s or master’s degree, which will be assessed by the Danish Research Agency if necessary.

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit their application online and include the following documents:

  • Cover letter explaining motivation and indicating whether applying for the 5+3 or 4+4 PhD program

  • Curriculum Vitae

  • Research project description (maximum 1,500 words)

  • Academic diplomas and transcripts (BA/BSc and MA/MSc)

  • Writing sample of article length

  • Optional documentation such as publications or English language qualifications

Applicants from universities where the teaching language is not English or a Scandinavian language must submit official translations of their academic documents.

Academic Environment at the Department of Political Science

The Department of Political Science at the University of Copenhagen is one of Europe’s top-ranked political science departments. It provides a dynamic and collaborative environment for ambitious researchers.

The department includes:

  • Around 75 faculty members

  • More than 10 tenure-track assistant professors

  • Approximately 25 PhD students

Research activities are organized into several research groups and centers, creating a rich intellectual community that embraces diverse theoretical and methodological approaches.

Inclusive and International Research Community

The University of Copenhagen is committed to creating an inclusive and equal opportunity working environment. The institution encourages applications from candidates of all backgrounds regardless of gender, ethnicity, age, disability, or sexual orientation.

International researchers also receive support services related to relocation, integration, and career counseling for accompanying partners, helping them transition smoothly into academic life in Denmark.

How to Apply

Interested candidates must submit their applications online through the University of Copenhagen recruitment portal.

Applications must be submitted before April 13, 2026, and late submissions will not be considered.

For more information click here 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post PhD Scholarship in Political Science at the University of Copenhagen: Researching Cancel Culture and Moral Judgement in Democratic Politics appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Google Creative Fellowship 2026: Fully Paid 6-Month Fellowship for Emerging Creatives at Google in the United States
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
The Google Creative Fellowship is a prestigious program designed to support and develop early-career creatives. Through real-world projects, mentorship, and collaboration with industry experts, the fellowship allows participants to work directly within Google’s in-house creative teams. If you are a designer, storyteller, filmmaker, technologist, or digital creative, this fellowship offers a unique opportunity to gain […]

The post Google Creative Fellowship 2026: Fully Paid 6-Month Fellowship for Emerging Creatives at Google in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Google Creative Fellowship is a prestigious program designed to support and develop early-career creatives. Through real-world projects, mentorship, and collaboration with industry experts, the fellowship allows participants to work directly within Google’s in-house creative teams. If you are a designer, storyteller, filmmaker, technologist, or digital creative, this fellowship offers a unique opportunity to gain hands-on experience at one of the world’s most influential technology companies.

Overview of the Google Creative Fellowship 2026

The Google Creative Fellowship 2026 is an exceptional career development opportunity designed for emerging creative professionals who want to gain hands-on experience working within one of the most influential technology companies in the world. The fellowship places selected participants directly into Google’s internal creative teams where they contribute to real projects, collaborate with world-class creatives, and build practical experience in the global technology and media landscape.

Unlike a traditional internship, the Creative Fellowship is structured as a six-month paid contractor role where participants work as freelancers through one of Google’s staffing partners. Fellows gain professional exposure, mentorship, networking opportunities, and the chance to collaborate on cutting-edge creative work that influences products and campaigns seen by millions of people worldwide.

The program is intended to cultivate a new generation of creative talent by providing a dynamic environment where fellows can experiment with innovative storytelling, design, technology, and content creation while contributing meaningfully to Google’s creative ecosystem.

Fellowship Duration and Program Timeline

The Google Creative Fellowship is a six-month program designed to provide immersive industry experience.

Program timeline:

  • Start Date: June 2026

  • End Date: December 2026

  • Duration: 6 months full-time

Fellows will work directly with Google’s creative teams during this period and participate in structured activities including mentorship sessions, project collaborations, orientation sessions, and creative talks from industry professionals.

Location of the Google Creative Fellowship

The fellowship is hosted in the United States and is conducted primarily on-site. Positions are available in several major cities including:

  • New York City

  • San Francisco

  • Los Angeles

Participants are expected to work in the office environment and collaborate directly with creative teams. The program is not fully remote, and fellows typically attend in-office work sessions at least several times per week.

Who Should Apply for the Google Creative Fellowship?

The fellowship is targeted at emerging creative thinkers who want to build a career in creative industries such as design, storytelling, digital media, technology, and advertising.

Google encourages applicants who:

  • Have 0–5 years of professional creative experience

  • Possess a strong creative perspective and portfolio

  • Demonstrate a passion for storytelling, design, technology, or digital creativity

  • Are eager to experiment with new tools, including emerging technologies like AI

  • Want to collaborate with world-class teams and build impactful creative work

Applicants should be passionate about making things, telling stories, and contributing unique ideas to large-scale creative campaigns and projects.

Countries Eligible for the Google Creative Fellowship

Although the fellowship is run by a global company, there are strict location requirements.

Eligible applicants must:

  • Be located in the United States

  • Have legal work authorization in the United States

  • Be available to work full-time from June 2026

This means the fellowship is technically open to applicants of any nationality, but they must already have the legal right to work in the United States.

Eligible regions:

  • Global applicants with U.S. work authorization

  • U.S. citizens or residents

  • Individuals already living and authorized to work in the United States

Relocation assistance is not provided, so applicants must be able to independently reside in the fellowship locations.

Fields and Roles Available in the Fellowship

The fellowship includes multiple creative roles across several of Google’s internal teams. Fellows can apply for up to two roles that best match their skills.

Below are some of the major creative areas available in the program:

1. Brand Studio Roles

The Brand Studio team at Google focuses on creating advertising campaigns, interactive experiences, films, and visual storytelling that represent Google’s brand globally.

Possible fellowship roles include:

  • Motion Designer

  • Producer

  • Social Creative / Copywriter

  • Creative Technologist

These roles involve tasks such as creating visual animations, managing production schedules, designing campaigns, writing social media content, and developing interactive digital experiences.

2. Creative Lab Roles

Google’s Creative Lab is responsible for exploring experimental ideas that communicate the future of Google products and innovations.

Available roles may include:

  • Creative Technical Motion Artist

  • Visual & Motion Designer

Participants in these roles often experiment with emerging technologies, including artificial intelligence tools, generative media, and experimental storytelling formats.

3. Google Cloud Brand & Creative Team

This team works on branding and storytelling for enterprise cloud products developed by Google.

Fellowship positions may include:

  • Creative Technologist

  • Social Creative

  • Visual & Motion Designer

These roles focus on transforming complex technology concepts into accessible and engaging creative narratives.

4. YouTube Creative Studio

The YouTube Creative Studio develops creative campaigns that showcase the platform and its creator ecosystem.

Roles may include:

  • Video Storyteller (AI Focus)

  • Writer / AI Prompt Artist

Participants work on social campaigns, brand storytelling, and content creation using modern digital tools.

Benefits of the Google Creative Fellowship

The Google Creative Fellowship offers numerous professional benefits that make it highly competitive.

1. Paid Fellowship

The program is fully paid, although the exact compensation depends on the location and will be communicated during the hiring process.

2. Hands-On Industry Experience

Fellows work on real creative projects within Google’s internal teams rather than simulated assignments.

3. Mentorship and Networking

Participants receive guidance from experienced professionals across Google’s creative departments and build networks that can significantly influence their future careers.

4. Professional Development

The fellowship includes:

  • Orientation programs

  • Creative talks and workshops

  • Collaborative projects

  • Peer community support

These components help fellows grow their creative skills and industry knowledge.

5. Exposure to Cutting-Edge Technologies

Many fellowship roles involve experimenting with technologies such as:

  • Artificial intelligence

  • Interactive design

  • Digital storytelling

  • Emerging creative tools

Qualifications Required for the Google Creative Fellowship

To be eligible for the fellowship, applicants generally need to meet the following qualifications:

Professional Experience

Applicants should have 0 to 5 years of experience in a creative field.

Portfolio or Demonstrated Work

Candidates must showcase a portfolio or body of work that demonstrates their creative abilities.

Relevant Skills

Depending on the role, skills may include:

  • Graphic design

  • Animation and motion design

  • Copywriting

  • Social media storytelling

  • Video production

  • Programming or creative coding

  • AI tools and generative media

Collaboration Skills

Applicants should demonstrate the ability to collaborate with cross-disciplinary teams.

What Fellows Will Do During the Program

Participants in the fellowship will work closely with Google’s creative teams and contribute to:

  • Brand campaigns

  • Video storytelling projects

  • Interactive media

  • Social media content

  • AI-driven creative experiments

  • Product storytelling initiatives

Fellows may work independently on projects or collaborate with designers, engineers, producers, and strategists to produce creative work at a global scale.

Why the Google Creative Fellowship Is Highly Competitive

The fellowship is considered one of the most prestigious early-career opportunities in the creative industry because it combines:

  • The reputation of Google

  • Direct industry experience

  • Paid employment

  • Access to cutting-edge creative tools

  • Mentorship from leading professionals

Because of these factors, thousands of creatives worldwide compete for a limited number of fellowship spots each year.

How to Apply for the Google Creative Fellowship 2026

APPLY NOW 

Applicants must submit their interest through the official Creative Fellowship website and select up to two roles that match their skillset.

Typical application components may include:

  • Portfolio submission

  • Resume or CV

  • Role selection

  • Personal details and background information

After submission, applicants receive confirmation emails and shortlisted candidates are contacted for further evaluation stages.

Apply here

Google Creative Fellowship 2026 Deadline

Applicants interested in the fellowship must submit their application before the official deadline:

Application Deadline: 23rd March 2026 (5:00 PM PST)

After the submission deadline, Google plans to notify shortlisted candidates by mid-April 2026, and finalist candidates will be contacted by a third-party employment agency in early May 2026 for further steps in the selection process.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Google Creative Fellowship 2026: Fully Paid 6-Month Fellowship for Emerging Creatives at Google in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: 2026 ECOSOC Youth Forum Speaking Opportunities: Apply to Speak at the United Nations (Open to youth Worldwide)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Young leaders from around the world can now apply for speaking opportunities at the 2026 ECOSOC Youth Forum, one of the most influential global platforms for youth engagement in sustainable development and global policy dialogue. The United Nations Economic and Social Council (ECOSOC) Youth Forum brings together young people, policymakers, UN officials, and civil society […]

The post 2026 ECOSOC Youth Forum Speaking Opportunities: Apply to Speak at the United Nations (Open to youth Worldwide) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young leaders from around the world can now apply for speaking opportunities at the 2026 ECOSOC Youth Forum, one of the most influential global platforms for youth engagement in sustainable development and global policy dialogue.

The United Nations Economic and Social Council (ECOSOC) Youth Forum brings together young people, policymakers, UN officials, and civil society organizations to discuss innovative solutions for advancing the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development and the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

Applications are currently open for youth speakers (both funded and self-funded) who are interested in sharing ideas, innovations, and youth-led initiatives during the Forum sessions.

Application Deadline: 7 March 2026, 8:00 AM EST
Forum Dates: 14–16 April 2026
Location: United Nations Headquarters, New York, United States
Youth Blast (capacity building activities): 13 April 2026

About the ECOSOC Youth Forum

The ECOSOC Youth Forum is the annual youth forum of the United Nations Economic and Social Council. It serves as a major platform for young people to contribute to global policy discussions and present ideas, innovations, and solutions that advance sustainable development.

The Forum brings together:

  • Youth leaders and youth organizations

  • Government representatives

  • United Nations agencies

  • Civil society organizations

  • Development practitioners and policymakers

Participants engage in discussions that help shape international policy conversations connected to the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development.

The Forum is closely linked to key UN processes, particularly the High-Level Political Forum on Sustainable Development (HLPF).

Theme of the 2026 ECOSOC Youth Forum

The theme for the 2026 ECOSOC Youth Forum is:

“Innovate, Unite, and Transform: Youth Shaping the Road to 2030.”

This theme aligns with the 2026 High-Level Political Forum on Sustainable Development, which focuses on transformative, equitable, innovative, and coordinated actions to accelerate progress toward the Sustainable Development Goals.

Sustainable Development Goals in Focus

The Forum will place special emphasis on the Sustainable Development Goals that will be reviewed at the 2026 High-Level Political Forum.

These include:

  • SDG 6 – Clean Water and Sanitation

  • SDG 7 – Affordable and Clean Energy

  • SDG 9 – Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure

  • SDG 11 – Sustainable Cities and Communities

  • SDG 17 – Partnerships for the Goals

The Forum will also contribute to discussions related to several upcoming global processes, including:

  • UN Water Conference 2026

  • World Urban Forum 2026 (WUF13)

  • SDG Summit 2027

  • UN-Habitat Assembly 2027

Speaking Opportunities at the ECOSOC Youth Forum

Young people are invited to apply to serve as speakers during the various sessions of the Forum.

Selected speakers will have the opportunity to:

  • Present youth-led solutions and initiatives related to the Sustainable Development Goals

  • Share research, innovations, and policy ideas

  • Contribute youth perspectives to global development discussions

  • Engage with policymakers, UN representatives, and global stakeholders

Youth speakers will also deliver short presentations between sessions where they can share personal experiences, innovative solutions, and insights related to sustainable development.

Applicants can use the application form to indicate:

  • Preferred session(s) they would like to speak in

  • Whether they are applying for funded or self-funded participation

  • Their relevant experience and expertise

Anyone selected for a speaking role will automatically receive an invitation to attend the Forum.

Format of the Forum

The 2026 ECOSOC Youth Forum will take place in a hybrid format, allowing participation both in person and online.

Key elements of the Forum include:

  • Opening plenary session

  • Closing plenary session

  • Interactive thematic sessions

  • Regional breakout sessions

  • Youth-led presentations

  • Networking and policy dialogue sessions

A Youth Blast event will take place on 13 April 2026, offering training, capacity building, and networking opportunities for participants before the main Forum begins.

Who Should Apply

Young people from around the world are encouraged to apply, particularly those who are working on issues related to sustainable development.

Potential applicants may include:

  • Youth activists and advocates

  • Young researchers and academics

  • Entrepreneurs and innovators

  • Civil society leaders

  • Youth organization representatives

  • Students and early career professionals

Applicants with experience in areas such as climate action, innovation, urban development, energy, water management, and partnerships for development are particularly encouraged to apply.

Selection Process

Applications will be reviewed by the youth co-leads coordinating the various Forum sessions.

Selection will be based on several factors, including:

  • Relevance of the applicant’s experience to the session theme

  • Quality of proposed contributions and ideas

  • Demonstrated leadership and engagement in sustainable development initiatives

  • Regional diversity and representation

Individuals interested in helping with the selection process can contact the organizing team via email.

Email: ecosocyf@unmgcy.org

How to Apply

Interested applicants can submit their applications through the official form.

Application link:
https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdk5xWqNC_7ih1a6PSO-KQG6zbPgcTxDoqWvnn68rWZy-MGpQ/viewform

Application deadline:
7 March 2026 at 8:00 AM EST

Learn More

More information about the Forum can be found on the official webpage.

Application link:
https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdk5xWqNC_7ih1a6PSO-KQG6zbPgcTxDoqWvnn68rWZy-MGpQ/viewform

ECOSOC Youth Forum page:
https://ecosoc.un.org/en/what-we-do/ecosoc-youth-forum/about-youth-forum/ecosoc-youth-forum-2026

Additional updates:
https://www.linktree.com/ecosocyouthforum

Application link:
https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdk5xWqNC_7ih1a6PSO-KQG6zbPgcTxDoqWvnn68rWZy-MGpQ/viewform

For more international fellowships, conferences, scholarships, and leadership opportunities for young people worldwide, visit www.opportunitiesforyouth.org.

The post 2026 ECOSOC Youth Forum Speaking Opportunities: Apply to Speak at the United Nations (Open to youth Worldwide) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: The Queen’s Commonwealth Writing Competition 2026: Global Writing Contest for Young Writers Across the Commonwealth
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The Queen’s Commonwealth Writing Competition (QCWC) 2026 is now officially open for submissions, inviting young writers from across the Commonwealth and beyond to share their creativity, ideas, and voices with the world. Organized by the Royal Commonwealth Society (RCS), the Queen’s Commonwealth Writing Competition is the world’s oldest international writing competition for schools, with a […]

The post The Queen’s Commonwealth Writing Competition 2026: Global Writing Contest for Young Writers Across the Commonwealth appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Queen’s Commonwealth Writing Competition (QCWC) 2026 is now officially open for submissions, inviting young writers from across the Commonwealth and beyond to share their creativity, ideas, and voices with the world.

Organized by the Royal Commonwealth Society (RCS), the Queen’s Commonwealth Writing Competition is the world’s oldest international writing competition for schools, with a remarkable history dating back to 1883. For more than a century, this prestigious global competition has provided a platform for young people to express their ideas, explore global themes, and develop their creative writing skills.

Each year, thousands of young writers from across the Commonwealth participate in the competition, submitting stories, essays, and other written works that reflect their perspectives on global issues and shared experiences.

For the 2026 edition, participants are invited to respond to the inspiring theme:

“Common Ground: Better Together.”

Young writers are encouraged to reflect on how shared values, cooperation, and collaboration can bring communities together and help address global challenges.

The competition deadline is 30 April 2026.

About The Queen’s Commonwealth Writing Competition

The Queen’s Commonwealth Writing Competition is one of the flagship youth initiatives of the Royal Commonwealth Society, an organization dedicated to promoting education, equality, sustainability, and global cooperation across Commonwealth nations.

Since its inception in the late 19th century, the competition has served as a powerful platform for young voices. It encourages participants to engage with global themes and express their ideas through creative writing.

Over the years, the competition has helped nurture generations of talented writers, many of whom have gone on to achieve remarkable accomplishments in literature, journalism, public service, and academia.

The competition has grown significantly in recent years. In 2025 alone, the competition received over 53,000 entries from around the world, demonstrating its global impact and the enthusiasm of young writers eager to share their perspectives.

Through this competition, the Royal Commonwealth Society seeks to empower youth by giving them the opportunity to explore important issues affecting their communities and the wider world.

2026 Theme: Common Ground – Better Together

The theme for the 2026 Queen’s Commonwealth Writing Competition is “Common Ground: Better Together.”

The Commonwealth is one of the most diverse international communities in the world, spanning multiple continents and representing a wide array of cultures, languages, landscapes, and generations.

Its member countries range from small island nations to vast and populous states. While these countries differ in geography, history, and cultural traditions, they are connected by shared values and common aspirations for a better future.

The theme encourages participants to explore questions such as:

  • What unites communities despite their differences?

  • How can cooperation and partnership drive positive change?

  • What shared challenges do Commonwealth nations face today?

  • How can young people contribute to building stronger and more inclusive communities?

Participants are encouraged to reflect creatively on how collaboration, mutual understanding, and shared responsibility can help address the challenges facing societies today.

Through storytelling, essays, poetry, or other creative writing forms, entrants can explore these ideas and share their unique perspectives.

Why This Competition Matters

The Queen’s Commonwealth Writing Competition plays a crucial role in encouraging young people to engage with global issues through creative expression.

Writing allows participants to:

  • Develop their creativity and communication skills

  • Reflect on social and global issues

  • Express their ideas and perspectives

  • Build confidence as young writers

  • Connect with peers across different cultures and countries

The competition also promotes literacy, critical thinking, and storytelling as tools for social awareness and personal development.

By participating in this global contest, young writers join a vibrant international community that celebrates creativity, diversity, and shared human values.

A Legacy of Distinguished Alumni

Over the years, many participants and winners of the Queen’s Commonwealth Writing Competition have gone on to achieve remarkable success in literature and beyond.

The competition has served as an early platform for talented young writers whose creativity and insight have shaped their future careers.

These alumni demonstrate how powerful storytelling and thoughtful writing can influence society, inspire change, and open doors to new opportunities.

The Royal Commonwealth Society celebrates the achievements of past participants who have used their voices to contribute to meaningful conversations about global issues.

The Evolution of the Competition

The Queen’s Commonwealth Writing Competition continues to evolve to reflect the changing landscape of global storytelling and youth engagement.

Formerly known as the Queen’s Commonwealth Essay Competition, the initiative has been reimagined to embrace a broader definition of writing and creativity.

This new identity reflects a more inclusive approach to storytelling, welcoming diverse forms of written expression such as:

  • Essays

  • Short stories

  • Poetry

  • Creative narratives

  • Reflective writing

This transformation ensures that the competition remains accessible, innovative, and relevant to young writers from all backgrounds.

How to Participate

Young writers who wish to participate in the competition must submit their entries through the official competition platform provided by the Royal Commonwealth Society.

Participants should carefully review the following before submitting their entries:

  • Official entry form

  • Competition guidelines

  • Submission requirements

  • Terms and conditions

  • Frequently asked questions

Resources are also available to help participants prepare their entries and understand the competition requirements.

All submissions must reflect the 2026 theme “Common Ground: Better Together.”

Global Reach of the Commonwealth Community

The Commonwealth represents one of the most diverse global communities, connecting countries across Africa, Asia, Europe, the Americas, and the Pacific.

Despite differences in culture, geography, and history, Commonwealth nations share common values rooted in cooperation, mutual respect, and a commitment to improving lives.

The Queen’s Commonwealth Writing Competition celebrates this diversity while encouraging young people to reflect on the shared values that unite communities.

By bringing together voices from across the Commonwealth, the competition highlights the importance of dialogue, understanding, and collaboration in shaping a better future.

Application Deadline

All entries for The Queen’s Commonwealth Writing Competition 2026 must be submitted by:

30 April 2026

Participants are encouraged to submit their entries before the deadline to ensure they meet all competition requirements.

Click HERE to learn more and apply.

For more global competitions, fellowships, grants, scholarships, conferences, and many others, subscribe to OFY.

The post The Queen’s Commonwealth Writing Competition 2026: Global Writing Contest for Young Writers Across the Commonwealth appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: African Commonwealth Students’ Convention 2026 in Uganda: Apply Now to Join Africa’s Next Generation of Student Leaders
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
Applications are now open for the African Commonwealth Students’ Convention 2026, a major gathering designed to bring together student leaders, youth innovators, policymakers, and institutional actors from across the African Commonwealth. The convention will take place in Uganda from April 12 to April 16, 2026, offering a unique platform for young changemakers to collaborate on […]

The post African Commonwealth Students’ Convention 2026 in Uganda: Apply Now to Join Africa’s Next Generation of Student Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the African Commonwealth Students’ Convention 2026, a major gathering designed to bring together student leaders, youth innovators, policymakers, and institutional actors from across the African Commonwealth.

The convention will take place in Uganda from April 12 to April 16, 2026, offering a unique platform for young changemakers to collaborate on solutions that advance Africa’s transformation through education, innovation, science, and technology.

Held under the framework of the Commonwealth Students’ Association (CSA), this convention provides an opportunity for students and youth leaders to share ideas, build networks, and contribute to discussions that influence the continent’s development agenda.

About the African Commonwealth Students’ Convention

The African Commonwealth Students’ Convention is designed to empower young people to play a central role in Africa’s development. It brings together students, youth leaders, innovators, and policymakers to exchange ideas and develop practical solutions to pressing challenges facing the continent.

Participants will engage in discussions, workshops, and collaborative sessions that focus on strengthening leadership, innovation, and policy engagement among African youth.

The convention also provides a valuable platform for delegates to showcase their ideas, initiatives, and projects aimed at creating positive change in their communities.

What the Convention Covers

Selected participants will receive support for all on-ground arrangements during the event. The convention will cover:

  • Accommodation throughout the convention

  • Meals during the program

  • Access to all sessions, workshops, and convention activities

  • Networking opportunities with leaders, innovators, and policymakers

Participants will only be required to cover their travel or flight costs to Uganda.

Key Thematic Areas

Applicants can select the thematic area that best aligns with their interests and experience.

The convention will focus on the following themes:

  • Education Reform

  • Innovation and Entrepreneurship

  • Science and Technology

  • Governance and Policy

  • Rural Youth Empowerment

  • Regional Integration

  • Other related areas (applicants may specify)

These themes are designed to encourage innovative thinking and collaborative problem-solving to address key challenges across Africa.

Benefits of Participating

The African Commonwealth Students’ Convention offers several benefits for participants, including:

  • Opportunities to network with student leaders and policymakers from across Africa

  • A platform to share ideas and innovative solutions

  • Exposure to discussions on education reform, technology, and governance

  • Leadership development and collaboration opportunities

  • Participation in workshops and policy-focused dialogues

Application Timeline

Applications are currently open for the African Commonwealth Students’ Convention 2026. While a specific application deadline has not been announced, applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible due to the competitive nature of the program.

The convention will take place from April 12 to April 16, 2026, in Uganda, and early application allows selected participants enough time to make travel arrangements.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should complete the official application through the convention’s application platform. Applicants are typically required to provide information about their leadership experience, interests, and preferred thematic area.

APPLY HERE. 

Early applications are strongly encouraged.

Discover more conference opportunities, click here. 

The post African Commonwealth Students’ Convention 2026 in Uganda: Apply Now to Join Africa’s Next Generation of Student Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Promise Venture Studio Opens $200K Build Award and $20K Imagine Award for Early Childhood Innovators
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
Early childhood innovators and community leaders now have a powerful opportunity to secure funding and support to scale their impact. Promise Venture Studio has launched two funding tracks through the Promise Fund designed to support founders working to improve outcomes for children ages 0–5 and the families and communities that support them. The two awards—the […]

The post Promise Venture Studio Opens $200K Build Award and $20K Imagine Award for Early Childhood Innovators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Early childhood innovators and community leaders now have a powerful opportunity to secure funding and support to scale their impact. Promise Venture Studio has launched two funding tracks through the Promise Fund designed to support founders working to improve outcomes for children ages 0–5 and the families and communities that support them.

The two awards—the Build Award and the Imagine Award—provide flexible funding, mentorship, and community support for early-stage innovators developing solutions that transform early childhood systems.

These opportunities aim to close a critical funding gap in early childhood innovation while empowering leaders who are deeply connected to the communities they serve.

Why Early Childhood Innovation Matters

Despite the enormous impact that early childhood investments can have on lifelong outcomes, the sector remains chronically underfunded. The Promise Fund was created to address this gap by investing in early-stage innovators and organizations focused on building equitable, community-rooted solutions.

Promise Venture Studio believes that leaders with strong community ties often hold the most powerful insights for transforming systems that serve young children and their families. By providing early capital and capacity-building support, the Promise Fund helps innovators test ideas, refine models, and grow sustainable organizations.

The program focuses specifically on solutions that benefit children ages 0–5, as well as parents, caregivers, and communities that nurture early development.

The Build Award: $200,000 for Early-Stage Early Childhood Organizations

The Build Award is designed for high-potential early-stage organizations that are ready to grow their impact. Selected organizations will receive $200,000 in unrestricted funding along with a 12-month partnership with Promise Venture Studio.

The award supports organizations led by community-connected founders who are working to create equitable outcomes for children and families.

Participants gain not only funding but also access to a comprehensive development program designed to strengthen organizational capacity and long-term sustainability.

What Build Award Recipients Receive

Successful applicants will benefit from a year-long program that combines funding, mentorship, and professional development support.

Key benefits include:

  • $200,000 in flexible funding to advance organizational goals

  • A 12-month strategic partnership with Promise Venture Studio

  • Direct one-on-one support from the Promise team

  • Personalized mentoring and expert guidance

  • Capacity-building support focused on growth and impact

  • In-person convenings for learning, collaboration, and reflection

  • Access to an ongoing alumni network and peer community

The program aims to help early-stage organizations strengthen their impact models, build sustainable strategies, and attract additional funding opportunities.

Build Award Deadline

The preliminary application window runs from March 2, 2026, to April 10, 2026.

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Organizations selected from the preliminary round will be invited to submit a full application due on July 2, 2026.

Applicants invited to complete the full application will receive an $800 honorarium for their time and effort.

Finalists will also have access to optional impact workshops designed to help them communicate their impact model and strengthen their funding readiness.

The Imagine Award: $20,000 for Idea-Stage Early Childhood Innovators

The Imagine Award supports early childhood changemakers who are at the idea stage and ready to test transformative concepts through real-world pilots.

The award provides $20,000 in flexible funding along with a six-month learning experience designed to help innovators refine their ideas and test them with real users.

Participants will develop practical skills in piloting solutions, collecting feedback, and refining their approaches to maximize impact.

What Imagine Award Participants Receive

Imagine Award recipients receive a combination of funding and tailored support to help them turn ideas into actionable pilots.

Program benefits include:

  • $20,000 in flexible grant funding

  • Six-month cohort-based learning program

  • One-on-one partnership and coaching from the Promise team

  • Mentorship from experienced leaders and experts

  • Cohort workshops focused on pilot design and implementation

  • A multi-day in-person convening for collaboration and learning

  • Continued alumni support and peer networking

Participants will also learn how to build effective feedback loops, test early solutions with users, and communicate their vision to attract future partnerships and funding.

Imagine Award Deadline

Applications for the Imagine Award will open during the week of July 6, 2026, and will close on August 28, 2026.

NOTIFY WHEN APPLICATIONS ARE OPEN HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Finalists will be notified during the week of September 28, 2026.

Applicants can also participate in free pilot workshops during July and August to design a low-cost or no-cost pilot plan that can be implemented within three to six months.

Investment Criteria

The Promise Fund evaluates applicants based on several key criteria that ensure projects align with its mission of advancing early childhood equity.

Focus on Early Childhood

Funded ideas and ventures must focus on children ages 0–5 and the parents, caregivers, and communities that support them.

Community-Connected Leadership

Priority is given to leaders who have direct connections to the communities they serve and whose lived experience informs their solutions.

Equity-Driven Solutions

Selected ventures must demonstrate a commitment to equitable outcomes for young children and their caregivers.

Long-Term Sustainability

Applicants should demonstrate a clear vision for sustainable impact and organizational growth.

Community-Centered Design

Solutions should be rooted in direct input from families and communities, ensuring programs meet real needs and priorities.

Why This Opportunity Stands Out

The Promise Fund represents a new model for equitable early-stage investment. By combining flexible funding with mentorship, peer learning, and capacity-building support, the program helps innovators move from ideas to scalable solutions.

For founders working to transform early childhood systems, these awards provide a rare opportunity to secure capital, mentorship, and a supportive network designed to accelerate impact.

Early childhood innovators with bold ideas and strong community ties are encouraged to apply and take advantage of this unique funding opportunity.

Discover more grant opportunities, click here. 

The post Promise Venture Studio Opens $200K Build Award and $20K Imagine Award for Early Childhood Innovators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now: Young Women’s Advisory Group Supporting Girls’ Access to Sport in Europe
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
Young women across Europe are invited to apply for the Young Women’s Advisory Group, a new initiative under the Playing for Purpose program by the adidas Foundation. The program aims to amplify the voices and experiences of young women to help shape initiatives that make sport and physical activity more inclusive and accessible. This opportunity […]

The post Apply Now: Young Women’s Advisory Group Supporting Girls’ Access to Sport in Europe appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young women across Europe are invited to apply for the Young Women’s Advisory Group, a new initiative under the Playing for Purpose program by the adidas Foundation. The program aims to amplify the voices and experiences of young women to help shape initiatives that make sport and physical activity more inclusive and accessible.

This opportunity is open to women aged 18–29 living in Germany, France, and Spain, especially those with multicultural or migration backgrounds and an interest in sport, youth empowerment, and community engagement.

About the Young Women’s Advisory Group

Sport should be a welcoming space for everyone. However, many girls and young women—particularly those from multicultural backgrounds living in Europe—still face barriers to participation. To address this, the adidas Foundation is launching the Young Women’s Advisory Group to ensure that young women’s voices, ideas, and lived experiences inform the development of programs designed to support girls and young women in sport.

Members of the advisory group will serve in a consultative role, providing insights, recommendations, and feedback that help guide the foundation’s upcoming project aimed at improving access to sports and encouraging girls to stay active.

While the foundation will make the final decisions, the perspectives and input from advisory group members will play an important role in shaping project design, implementation, and evaluation.

What Participation Involves

The advisory group is designed to be flexible and manageable alongside studies, work, and personal commitments.

Participants can expect:

  • Online meetings and discussions

  • In-person workshops, including one event in Germany

  • Around two hours of commitment per month

  • Activities scheduled in advance with clearly defined expectations

Advisory group members will receive a fixed allowance in recognition of their time, expertise, and lived experiences. To ensure participation is accessible, the adidas Foundation will also cover travel, accommodation, and related costs for in-person meetings.

Who Should Apply

This opportunity may be a great fit if you:

  • Live in **Germany, France, or Spain

  • Identify as a woman aged 18–29

  • Have experience with or an interest in sport

  • Work with, support, or care about young people

  • Bring perspectives shaped by multicultural or migration backgrounds

Applicants who are passionate about making sport more inclusive and empowering for young women are strongly encouraged to apply.

Application Process

Interested candidates should first review the full Call for Applications to learn more about the role, expectations, and selection process.

How to Apply

  1. Read the full Call for Applications

  2. Complete the online application form

The application will require basic information along with a few short responses about your motivation, experience, and interest in joining the advisory group.

Shortlisted applicants may be invited for a brief online conversation. All applicants will be notified once the selection process is complete.

Deadline

22 March 2026 at 23:59 CET

Click here to discover more opportunities.

The post Apply Now: Young Women’s Advisory Group Supporting Girls’ Access to Sport in Europe appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: AmaliTech AWS re/Start Programme 2026: Fully Funded Cloud Computing Training for Ghanaian STEM Graduates (Free Certification, Stipends, and Job Pathways)
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
The demand for cloud computing professionals is growing rapidly across the world. Organizations in every industry—from finance and healthcare to e-commerce and telecommunications—are moving their infrastructure to the cloud. To address this demand and empower young professionals with future-ready skills, AmaliTech collaborates with Amazon Web Services to deliver the AWS re/Start Programme, a career-focused training […]

The post AmaliTech AWS re/Start Programme 2026: Fully Funded Cloud Computing Training for Ghanaian STEM Graduates (Free Certification, Stipends, and Job Pathways) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The demand for cloud computing professionals is growing rapidly across the world. Organizations in every industry—from finance and healthcare to e-commerce and telecommunications—are moving their infrastructure to the cloud. To address this demand and empower young professionals with future-ready skills, AmaliTech collaborates with Amazon Web Services to deliver the AWS re/Start Programme, a career-focused training initiative designed to prepare individuals for entry-level roles in cloud computing.

Introduction

The AmaliTech AWS re/Start Programme is a fully funded cloud computing training initiative designed to equip unemployed or underemployed graduates with in-demand digital skills for careers in cloud technology. The programme is implemented by AmaliTech in partnership with Amazon Web Services (AWS) through the global AWS re/Start Programme initiative.

This highly competitive technology training programme provides participants with the opportunity to gain hands-on experience in cloud computing, prepare for globally recognized AWS certifications, and develop professional skills needed to secure employment in the rapidly growing tech industry.

The programme is specifically targeted at Ghanaian graduates who are passionate about launching a career in cloud computing but may not yet have the industry experience required to enter the technology workforce.

By offering structured learning, career coaching, certification preparation, and job-readiness training, the programme serves as a bridge between academic knowledge and real-world employment opportunities in the digital economy.

Overview of the AWS re/Start Programme

The AWS re/Start Programme is a global workforce development initiative designed to prepare individuals for entry-level cloud computing careers. It focuses on providing learners with foundational IT knowledge, practical cloud skills, and career support that can help them secure jobs in technology and IT support roles.

In collaboration with AmaliTech, the programme has been adapted to empower young African professionals with the skills needed to compete in the global technology workforce.

Participants undergo intensive training that covers key aspects of cloud computing, networking, programming, databases, and cybersecurity. By the end of the programme, learners are equipped with the technical and professional competencies required to pursue roles such as:

  • Cloud support associate

  • Technical support specialist

  • Systems administrator

  • IT support engineer

  • Cloud operations associate

These roles are among the fastest-growing career paths in the global technology sector.

Programme Duration and Structure

The AmaliTech AWS re/Start Programme is structured as a 12-week intensive training programme delivered entirely online.

Participants are required to commit to a full-time learning schedule, dedicating approximately 8 hours per day from Monday to Friday to the programme.

The programme is delivered in cohorts, meaning applicants are grouped into batches that begin training at specific times during the year.

2026 Cohorts

  • January – April

  • April – July

  • July – October

This cohort-based structure ensures that participants benefit from collaborative learning, mentorship, and peer networking throughout the programme.

Benefits of the AmaliTech AWS re/Start Programme

Participants selected for the programme receive a wide range of benefits that go beyond technical training.

1. Free Cloud Computing Training

The programme is completely free, allowing participants to access high-quality cloud computing education without financial barriers.

2. AWS Certification Exam Voucher

Participants receive a voucher to take an AWS certification exam, enabling them to obtain a globally recognized credential that enhances their employability.

3. Stipends and Internet Support

To ensure learners can fully participate in the programme, selected candidates receive stipends and internet bundles to support their studies.

4. Industry-Relevant Curriculum

The curriculum is developed and delivered by experts and aligns with current industry standards and employer expectations.

5. Professional Network Development

Participants gain access to a growing community of instructors, mentors, and technology professionals who can provide career guidance and opportunities.

6. Soft Skills and Career Preparation

In addition to technical training, learners develop critical professional skills such as:

  • Communication and teamwork

  • Resume and CV development

  • Interview preparation

  • Career planning

These skills significantly improve participants’ chances of securing employment after completing the programme.

Skills and Knowledge You Will Learn

The programme provides comprehensive training in several key areas of cloud computing and IT infrastructure.

Cloud Computing Fundamentals

Participants begin by learning the fundamentals of cloud computing and how modern organizations use cloud platforms to manage applications and data.

Linux and System Administration

Learners gain practical experience with Linux operating systems, which are widely used in cloud environments.

Networking

The programme covers essential networking concepts such as:

  • IP addressing

  • Network architecture

  • Routing and switching

  • Network security

Python Programming

Participants learn the basics of Python programming, enabling them to automate tasks and manage cloud infrastructure.

Database Fundamentals

Training includes an introduction to database systems and how they are deployed in cloud environments.

AWS Core Services

Students gain practical knowledge of key AWS services used by organizations worldwide.

This combination of skills prepares participants for real-world IT and cloud support roles.

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for the AmaliTech AWS re/Start Programme, applicants must meet specific eligibility requirements.

Nationality Requirement

Applicants must be Ghanaian citizens living in Ghana.

Educational Qualification

Candidates must hold a tertiary degree in a STEM-related field, such as:

  • Computer Science

  • Information Technology

  • Engineering

  • Mathematics or related disciplines

Employment Status

Applicants must currently be:

  • Unemployed, or

  • Underemployed and seeking a career in cloud computing.

Technical Requirements

Applicants must have:

  • Access to a computer or laptop with webcam and microphone

  • A reliable internet connection for online training sessions.

Commitment Requirement

Candidates must be able to participate in full-time training sessions for 8 hours daily from Monday to Friday during the programme duration.

Passion for Cloud Computing

Applicants should demonstrate strong motivation to pursue a career in cloud technology.

Career Opportunities After Completing the Programme

Graduates of the AWS re/Start Programme are well-positioned to pursue entry-level roles in the cloud computing and IT sectors.

Possible career paths include:

  • Cloud Support Associate

  • IT Support Engineer

  • Systems Administrator

  • DevOps Support Engineer

  • Technical Operations Specialist

Because cloud computing continues to grow globally, professionals with AWS skills are increasingly in demand across industries.

Why the AWS re/Start Programme Is a Valuable Opportunity

The global demand for cloud computing professionals has increased dramatically as organizations migrate their operations to cloud platforms.

By combining:

  • industry-recognized certification preparation,

  • hands-on technical training,

  • professional development support, and

  • connections to potential employers,

Application and Selection Process

The selection process for the programme is designed to identify motivated candidates with the potential to succeed in cloud computing careers.

Step 1: Online Application

Interested candidates must complete the official application form through the programme’s website. Apply here

Step 2: Application Screening

Submitted applications are reviewed to ensure candidates meet the eligibility criteria.

Step 3: Phone Interview

Shortlisted candidates participate in a phone interview to assess motivation and suitability for the programme.

Step 4: Learner Assessment

Applicants complete an assessment sent via email to evaluate their aptitude for the programme.

Step 5: Admission Notification

Successful candidates receive an official email confirming their admission into the programme.

Application Deadline

31st March 2026

The AmaliTech AWS re/Start Programme provides a comprehensive pathway for young graduates to transition into high-demand technology careers.For many participants, the programme serves as the first step toward a long-term career in cloud computing and digital infrastructure.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post AmaliTech AWS re/Start Programme 2026: Fully Funded Cloud Computing Training for Ghanaian STEM Graduates (Free Certification, Stipends, and Job Pathways) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
